title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Authentic Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Perfection
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Lamb Ouzi with our whole slow roasted recipe. A traditional Middle Eastern dish, perfect for special occasions. Get step-by-step instructions!

authentic Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi recipe
whole Lamb Ouzi recipe step by step

best whole lamb ouzi at home
seo.h1: Authentic Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Lamb
h2Headings: Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
Step-by-Step Guide to Making Lamb Ouzi
Tips for the Best Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
seo.imageAlt: A beautifully presented whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi, a traditional Middle Eastern dish with fragrant rice and nuts.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich flavors of this authentic Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi, slow-roasted to perfection for an unforgettable meal.

description: Lamb Ouzi is a magnificent Middle Eastern dish featuring succulent whole slow roasted lamb, infused with aromatic spices, and served over a bed of fragrant spiced rice. This impressive recipe is perfect for grand celebrations, offering a truly unforgettable culinary experience that embodies the rich flavors of the region.

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine yogurt, 1/4 cup olive oil, minced garlic, 1 tablespoon baharat, cumin, coriander, cardamom, 1/2 teaspoon turmeric, cinnamon, salt, and black pepper. Generously rub this marinade all over the whole lamb shoulder or leg, ensuring it gets into any crevices. Place the lamb in a roasting pan, cover, and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to penetrate deeply.

instructions.title: Sear the Lamb
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 160C (325F). In a large, oven-safe roasting pan, heat a little extra olive oil over medium-high heat on the stovetop. Sear the marinated lamb on all sides until beautifully browned, about 3-4 minutes per side. This step locks in the juices and adds depth of flavor to the lamb.

instructions.title: Slow Roast the Lamb
instructions.text: Add about 1 cup of water or chicken broth to the bottom of the roasting pan. Cover the pan tightly with heavy-duty foil or a lid. Transfer to the preheated oven and slow roast for 3 to 3.5 hours, or until the lamb is fork-tender and easily pulls away from the bone. Baste occasionally with the pan juices. For the last 30 minutes of cooking, remove the foil to allow the lamb to brown further and achieve a beautiful crust.

instructions.title: Prepare the Ouzi Rice Base
instructions.text: While the lamb rests after roasting, rinse the basmati rice thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt a knob of butter or heat a tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat. Add the broken vermicelli noodles and cook, stirring constantly, until they turn golden brown. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Cook the Rice and Toast Nuts
instructions.text: Stir the rinsed basmati rice into the pot with the vermicelli and onions. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon baharat, 1/2 teaspoon turmeric, and season with salt and pepper to taste. Pour in the 5 cups of chicken broth and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot tightly, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is fluffy. Meanwhile, in a separate small pan, toast the pine nuts and slivered almonds in a little butter until golden brown. If using, soak raisins in warm water for 10 minutes then drain.

instructions.title: Assemble and Serve
instructions.text: Fluff the cooked Ouzi rice with a fork and gently fold in the toasted nuts and drained raisins. Transfer the fragrant Ouzi rice to a large, decorative serving platter. Carefully place the whole slow roasted lamb on top of the bed of rice. Garnish generously with fresh chopped parsley or mint for color and freshness. Serve immediately with a side of plain yogurt or a simple cucumber and tomato salad.

tips: For truly tender lamb, ensure the roasting pan is tightly sealed with foil to trap steam, allowing the meat to cook slowly and evenly.
Do not skip searing the lamb before roasting; it develops a rich crust and deepens the overall flavor of the dish.
The Ouzi rice can be prepared slightly in advance and gently reheated. Always toast your nuts just before serving to maintain their crispness and flavor.
article:
Prepare to embark on a culinary journey that promises to transport your taste buds straight to the heart of the Middle East. Among the many jewels of Arabian cuisine, Lamb Ouzi stands out as a true masterpiece – a dish synonymous with celebration, hospitality, and an unforgettable depth of flavor. While there are various interpretations, the whole slow-roasted version is arguably the most majestic, transforming a humble cut of lamb into an incredibly tender, aromatic centerpiece. This article will guide you through crafting a spectacular Lamb Ouzi Recipe, perfect for impressing guests at your next special gathering or simply indulging in a rich, comforting meal. Get ready to discover the secrets to a truly authentic Middle Eastern dinner experience.

## What is Lamb Ouzi?

Lamb Ouzi, sometimes spelled "Ozzie" or "Ouzi," is a quintessential Middle Eastern dish, revered for its grandeur and deliciousness. At its core, it features succulent, slow-cooked lamb, often served atop a bed of fragrant, spiced rice, studded with toasted nuts and sometimes peas or other vegetables. While some versions might involve small individual lamb shanks or cubed lamb, the "whole slow roasted" approach elevates it to a celebratory feast, where a large cut of lamb (like a leg or shoulder) is cooked until it’s fall-off-the-bone tender. This particular Lamb Ouzi Recipe focuses on achieving that incredible tenderness and rich flavor through a patient slow-roasting method, ensuring every bite is a delight. It's more than just a meal; it's an experience, a symbol of generosity and the rich tapestry of Middle Eastern culinary traditions.

## Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

Crafting the perfect Lamb Ouzi requires a thoughtful selection of ingredients, each playing a crucial role in building its complex flavor profile. This detailed list will set you up for success with our Lamb Ouzi Recipe.

**For the Whole Lamb:**
*   1 whole bone-in leg of lamb or shoulder (approx. 5-7 lbs / 2.3-3.2 kg)
*   2 tablespoons olive oil
*   6-8 cloves garlic, minced
*   1/4 cup fresh lemon juice
*   2 teaspoons salt
*   1 teaspoon black pepper
*   1 tablespoon ground cumin
*   1 tablespoon ground coriander
*   1 teaspoon ground turmeric
*   1 teaspoon paprika
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cardamom
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon
*   Pinch of saffron threads (optional, steeped in 2 tbsp hot water)
*   2 cups lamb or chicken broth (for the roasting pan)

**For the Spiced Rice:**
*   3 cups basmati rice, rinsed until water runs clear
*   2 tablespoons olive oil or ghee
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   4 cloves garlic, minced
*   1 teaspoon ground cumin
*   1 teaspoon ground coriander
*   1/2 teaspoon ground turmeric
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon
*   1/4 teaspoon ground cardamom
*   1/4 teaspoon black pepper
*   1/4 teaspoon allspice
*   1 bay leaf
*   4.5 cups lamb or chicken broth
*   1 teaspoon salt (or to taste)
*   1/2 cup frozen green peas (optional)
*   1/4 cup golden raisins or dried cranberries (optional)

**For Garnish:**
*   1/2 cup pine nuts, toasted
*   1/2 cup slivered almonds, toasted
*   Fresh parsley or mint, chopped (for garnish)

## Step-by-Step Guide to Making Lamb Ouzi

Preparing this magnificent Lamb Ouzi Recipe might seem daunting, but by breaking it down into manageable steps, you'll find it a rewarding and enjoyable process.

1.  **Prepare the Lamb Marinade:** In a small bowl, combine the olive oil, minced garlic, lemon juice, salt, pepper, cumin, coriander, turmeric, paprika, cardamom, cinnamon, and optional steeped saffron. Mix well to form a thick paste.
2.  **Marinate the Lamb:** Pat the lamb leg thoroughly dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make several deep incisions all over the lamb. Rub the marinade generously into the lamb, ensuring it gets into the incisions. Place the lamb in a large roasting pan, cover tightly with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to penetrate deeply.
3.  **Pre-heat Oven & Prepare for Roasting:** Preheat your oven to a low temperature, around 300°F (150°C). Remove the lamb from the refrigerator and let it come to room temperature for about 30-60 minutes. Pour the 2 cups of lamb or chicken broth into the bottom of the roasting pan.
4.  **Slow Roast the Lamb:** Cover the roasting pan tightly with heavy-duty aluminum foil or a lid. Place it in the preheated oven and slow roast for 4-6 hours, or until the lamb is incredibly tender and easily pulls away from the bone. The exact time will depend on the size of your lamb and your oven. For the last 30-45 minutes, remove the foil to allow the lamb to brown and crisp up slightly.
5.  **Rest the Lamb:** Once cooked, remove the lamb from the oven, transfer it to a cutting board, and tent it loosely with foil. Let it rest for at least 20-30 minutes. This is crucial for retaining its juices and tenderness. While the lamb rests, you can prepare the rice.
6.  **Prepare the Spiced Rice:** While the lamb is resting, heat olive oil or ghee in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
7.  **Toast Rice & Spices:** Add the rinsed basmati rice to the pot along with the cumin, coriander, turmeric, cinnamon, cardamom, black pepper, allspice, and bay leaf. Stir constantly for 2-3 minutes, toasting the rice and spices until fragrant.
8.  **Cook the Rice:** Pour in the lamb or chicken broth and add the salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot tightly, and simmer for 15-18 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender. If using, stir in the green peas and golden raisins during the last 5 minutes of cooking. Once cooked, remove from heat and let it sit, covered, for 5-10 minutes before fluffing with a fork.
9.  **Toast Nuts & Garnish:** While the rice cooks, lightly toast the pine nuts and slivered almonds in a dry pan over medium-low heat until golden brown and fragrant. Be careful, as they can burn quickly.
10. **Assemble and Serve:** To serve, create a large mound of the spiced rice on a big serving platter. Carefully shred or carve the tender lamb and arrange it generously over the rice. Garnish with the toasted pine nuts, slivered almonds, and freshly chopped parsley or mint. Your impressive Lamb Ouzi Recipe is now ready to be devoured!

## Tips for the Best Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

Achieving perfection with your Lamb Ouzi Recipe is all about attention to detail. Here are some invaluable tips to ensure your dish is nothing short of spectacular:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the lamb sits in the spice rub, the more flavorful and tender it will become. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Low and Slow is the Way to Go:** Patience is a virtue when slow roasting. A low oven temperature over many hours breaks down the tough fibers in the lamb, resulting in incredibly tender, melt-in-your-mouth meat.
*   **Keep it Moist:** Covering the roasting pan tightly with foil or a lid traps steam, helping to keep the lamb moist throughout the long cooking process. A little broth in the pan also contributes to this.
*   **Rest the Meat:** Always, always rest the lamb after it comes out of the oven. This allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring it stays succulent when carved.
*   **Flavorful Broth:** Use good quality lamb or chicken broth for both the roasting pan and the rice. It significantly enhances the overall depth of flavor.
*   **Don't Overcook the Rice:** Basmati rice cooks relatively quickly. Stick to the recommended cooking times and avoid lifting the lid too often, as this releases steam essential for proper cooking.
*   **Toasted Nuts for Texture:** Toasting the nuts just before serving adds a crucial textural contrast and a delightful nutty aroma that completes the dish.

## Serving Suggestions for Lamb Ouzi

Lamb Ouzi is a feast in itself, but traditional accompaniments can elevate the dining experience even further. Serve your Lamb Ouzi Recipe traditionally on a large communal platter, allowing guests to serve themselves from the abundance.

*   **Yogurt Sauce:** A simple, refreshing yogurt sauce made with plain Greek yogurt, minced garlic, and fresh mint or cucumber is a classic pairing. Its tanginess cuts through the richness of the lamb beautifully.
*   **Fresh Salads:** Light, vibrant Middle Eastern salads like Fattoush (with fried pita bread, mixed greens, and sumac dressing) or Tabbouleh (a parsley and bulgur salad) offer a fresh contrast.
*   **Pickles:** Assorted pickled vegetables, such as turnips, cucumbers, or olives, provide a briny, acidic counterpoint that cleanses the palate.
*   **Warm Bread:** Freshly baked pita bread or other flatbreads are perfect for scooping up any remaining rice or sauces.
*   **Roasted Vegetables:** Simple roasted vegetables like carrots, potatoes, or bell peppers can be served alongside for extra color and nutrition.

## Variations & Substitutions for Lamb Ouzi

While this Lamb Ouzi Recipe provides a fantastic authentic base, feel free to experiment and adapt it to your preferences or what you have on hand.

*   **Lamb Cut:** If a whole leg is too large, you can use bone-in lamb shoulder or even large lamb shanks, adjusting cooking time accordingly. The "whole slow roasted" aspect might change, but the flavors will remain.
*   **Spice Profile:** Adjust the spices to your liking. For more heat, add a pinch of cayenne pepper or chili flakes. For a more intense aromatic profile, consider adding cloves or a star anise to the rice.
*   **Rice Add-ins:** Beyond peas and raisins, consider adding chickpeas, lentils, or even finely diced carrots to the rice for extra texture and flavor. Dried apricots or prunes can also be a delightful sweet-savory addition.
*   **Cooking Method:** For smaller cuts of lamb, you could achieve tenderness using a large Dutch oven on the stovetop (braising) or even a pressure cooker for a quicker result, though the deep roasted flavor will be different.
*   **Stuffing:** A more elaborate variation of Lamb Ouzi involves deboning a lamb shoulder and stuffing it with the spiced rice mixture before rolling and roasting. This creates a spectacular presentation.
*   **Vegetarian Option:** While not true Lamb Ouzi, the spiced rice itself is incredibly flavorful and can be served as a vegetarian main dish by adding more vegetables, chickpeas, and perhaps some halloumi cheese.

This Lamb Ouzi Recipe is more than just instructions; it’s an invitation to create a memorable culinary experience. The effort is minimal compared to the incredible reward of a truly tender, aromatic, and deeply satisfying dish that will transport everyone at your table to the vibrant world of Middle Eastern flavors. Enjoy the process, and savor every magnificent bite!
